title: Give an LLM Access to External APIs (Function Calling)
taskDesc: Implement secure function-calling access so an LLM can invoke approved external APIs for Hesperia’s customer support workflow. The integration must support controlled tool selection, validated inputs, and reliable responses for production use.

instructions.tip: Keep the contract surface area intentionally small and explicit. Every field should have a clear purpose, a strict type, and a predictable response shape so downstream routing and validation stay deterministic and easy to audit.
instructions.desc: Create the function-calling interface for Hesperia Assist by defining the exact tools the model may invoke for customer support tasks. The tool set must be limited to the approved Hesperia APIs for order lookup, subscription lookup, and knowledge-base search, with clear input schemas, required fields, and response expectations for each function. Ensure the contracts are precise enough for production use and reflect the support-agent workflow used by Hesperia’s mid-market e-commerce and subscription customers.
instructions.goal: Specify the external API functions the Hesperia Assist LLM is allowed to call.
instructions.title: Define Tool Contracts
deliverables: Tool schema definitions in JSON or equivalent machine-readable format
Function signatures for each approved Hesperia API tool
Input validation rules for all tool parameters
taskToComplete: Define only the approved Hesperia support tools and exclude any unapproved actions.
Specify strict input parameters, required fields, and expected output structure for each tool.
Include validation rules that prevent malformed or unsafe requests from reaching Hesperia APIs.
instructions.dataGenerationPrompt: Generate a comprehensive mock support-tool dataset for Hesperia Assist that includes representative order lookup, subscription lookup, and knowledge-base search examples. Include all fields needed across the full workflow: tool names, allowed parameters, required vs optional fields, valid sample values, invalid/malformed values, boundary cases, missing-field cases, and example API responses for success and failure. Ensure the dataset covers customer identifiers, order IDs, subscription IDs, search queries, timestamps, status values, error codes, and any validation edge cases needed for contract definition, routing, security testing, and end-to-end validation.

instructions.tip: Treat routing as a policy decision, not just a parsing task. Make sure the model’s output is validated against the approved tool list before any execution path is allowed to continue.
instructions.desc: Build the function-calling flow that lets Hesperia Assist decide when to use an external API and which approved tool to call based on the support request. The routing logic must handle customer order questions, subscription questions, and knowledge-base queries without exposing the model to arbitrary endpoints. Include deterministic handling for tool invocation, response parsing, and fallback behavior when the model does not provide a valid function call.
instructions.goal: Wire the LLM to select and execute the correct Hesperia API function.
instructions.title: Implement Call Routing
deliverables: Tool invocation and response parsing logic
Fallback handling for invalid or missing tool calls
taskToComplete: Route only to the approved Hesperia tool set and reject any unsupported function request.
Parse model-generated arguments and pass only validated values to the external API layer.
Define fallback behavior for missing, invalid, or ambiguous function-call outputs.

instructions.tip: Design security controls so they fail closed. If authentication, request context, or tool intent is uncertain, the safest behavior is to stop the call and surface a controlled error path.
instructions.desc: Add the security and reliability controls required for Hesperia Assist to safely access external APIs in a live support environment. The implementation must include authentication handling, secret management, request timeouts, retry behavior, and safeguards against prompt injection or unauthorized tool use. Make sure the integration is suitable for support agents who may trigger API calls during active customer conversations.
instructions.goal: Protect Hesperia’s external API integrations with production-grade controls.
instructions.title: Secure API Access
deliverables: Timeout and retry settings for external calls
taskToComplete: Use secure credential handling for all Hesperia API access and avoid hard-coded secrets.
Apply request timeout and retry rules appropriate for live support usage.
Add guardrails that block unsafe tool use and reduce prompt-injection risk.

instructions.tip: Include both happy-path and negative-path tests, and verify the exact shape of the returned payloads. Production issues often come from edge cases where the tool call succeeds but the response is incomplete, ambiguous, or unsafe to display.
instructions.desc: Test the full function-calling path from user request to LLM tool selection to external API response handling for Hesperia Assist. Cover successful order lookups, subscription lookups, knowledge-base searches, and failure scenarios such as invalid inputs, unavailable APIs, and unsupported requests. Confirm that the integration returns structured, support-ready outputs that fit Hesperia’s customer service workflow.
instructions.goal: Verify that Hesperia Assist can reliably call external APIs through function calling.
instructions.title: Validate End-To-End
deliverables: End-to-end test cases or test suite
Sample request and response payloads for each supported tool
Validation results for success and failure scenarios
taskToComplete: Test at least one successful case for each approved Hesperia tool.
Test invalid input, API failure, and unsupported-request scenarios.
Verify that returned data is structured and safe for support-agent consumption.

taskContent.taskScenario: Hesperia is a SaaS company that sells Hesperia Assist, an AI support copilot used by mid-market customer service teams. The product team wants the copilot to fetch order status, subscription details, and knowledge-base answers from approved external APIs so agents can resolve tickets faster for e-commerce and subscription customers. Your job is to implement the function-calling layer that safely connects the LLM to these APIs while keeping the experience reliable for support agents handling live customer requests.

taskLearnings:
## What You Will Learn

### Tool-Calling Contracts
You will learn how to design **strict, machine-readable tool schemas** that constrain an LLM to a small approved action set. This includes understanding required fields, parameter types, response shapes, and why narrow contracts improve reliability and auditability.

### Routing and Control Flow
You will learn the principles behind **intent-to-tool routing**: how model output is interpreted, validated, and mapped to a safe execution path. You’ll also learn why fallback behavior is a core part of production function calling.

### Security and Trust Boundaries
You will learn how to think about **tool access as a security boundary**, including least privilege, fail-closed behavior, secret handling, and defenses against malformed or adversarial prompts.

### Validation and Test Coverage
You will learn how to reason about **happy paths, negative paths, and edge cases** for external API integrations.

| Area | Core Concept |
|---|---|
| Contracts | Deterministic schemas and allowed actions |
| Routing | Safe selection of the correct tool |
| Security | Guardrails, validation, and controlled failure |
| Testing | Success, invalid input, and unsupported requests |

### Production Readiness
You will learn how these pieces combine into a support workflow that is predictable, safe, and suitable for real customer-facing LLM systems.

submissionFiles.title: Tool Contract Schema
submissionFiles.desc: Machine-readable definitions for the approved Hesperia function-calling tools, including schemas, required fields, response expectations, and validation rules.

submissionFiles.title: Router and Guardrail Implementation
submissionFiles.desc: A single Git repository containing the routing logic, tool invocation flow, validation layer, and security guardrails for approved API access.

submissionFiles.title: End-to-End Validation Suite
submissionFiles.desc: Structured test cases covering successful tool calls, invalid inputs, unsupported requests, and API failure scenarios.
